content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN and a positive integer XXX. You are allowed to perform the following operation **at most once**:


Choose any contiguous subarray of AAA and multiply each element of the subarray by XXX.
Your task is to determine the maximum possible frequency of any element in the array after performing the operation at most once. The frequency of an element is defined as the number of times it appears in the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the length of array and the multiplier, respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_{N}A1​,A2​,...,AN​ — the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer — the maximum frequency of any element that can be achieved after performing the operation at most once.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^{5}2⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Multiplying any subarray by X=1X=1X=1 leaves the array unchanged. Therefore, no operation is needed, and the maximum frequency remains 111.
Test Case 2:
[10,2,8,2,4]→Choose Substring from l=2 to r=5[10,10,40,10,20][10, 2, 8, 2, 4] \xrightarrow{\text{Choose Substring from }l=2 \text{ to } r=5} [10, 10, 40, 10, 20][10,2,8,2,4]Choose Substring from l=2 to r=5​[10,10,40,10,20]. It can be shown that there is no way to achieve a maximum frequency greater than 333.
Test Case 3:
[6,2,6,2]→Choose Substring from l=2 to r=4[6,6,18,6][6, 2, 6, 2] \xrightarrow{\text{Choose Substring from }l=2 \text{ to } r=4} [6, 6, 18, 6][6,2,6,2]Choose Substring from l=2 to r=4​[6,6,18,6]. It can be shown that there is no way to achieve a maximum frequency greater than 333.
Test Case 4:
[5,3,2,5,4]→Choose Substring from l=3 to r=3[5,3,4,5,4][5, 3, 2, 5, 4] \xrightarrow{\text{Choose Substring from }l=3 \text{ to } r=3} [5, 3, 4, 5, 4][5,3,2,5,4]Choose Substring from l=3 to r=3​[5,3,4,5,4].
The frequency of both 555 and 444 is 222, and it can be shown that no operation can yield a maximum frequency greater than 222
